extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping pallor from false to true (all other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity. Pallor influences the output through exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest level) when pallor AND all nine other symptoms (vision_loss, focal_weakness, sweating, hemoptysis, syncope, chest_pain, palpitations, vomiting, confusion) are true. No rule raises acuity based on pallor, so pallor can only ever drive the output downward via this dominating-priority drop rule.

Counterexample comparison with all other inputs held fixed at: vision_loss=T, focal_weakness=T, sweating=T, hemoptysis=T, syncope=T, chest_pain=T, palpitations=T, vomiting=T, confusion=T, back_pain=F.

Baseline (pallor=FALSE): interaction_drop's conjunction is false, so it does not fire. The priority-3 raise rules (focal_weakness, chest_pain, vomiting, hemoptysis) fire and set acuity := emergent; lower-priority urgent rules are dominated. Result = emergent.

Witness (pallor=TRUE): now interaction_drop's full 10-way conjunction holds. At priority 100 it dominates every other matching rule (all priority 2 or 3) and sets acuity := self_care. Result = self_care.

Setting pallor true changes acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest) — a strict lowering. Therefore the monotonicity property is VIOLATED. The witness_assignment gives the pallor=true input that produces the lowered acuity; the corresponding pallor=false input yields emergent.
